channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: It’s a bad time to want a new computer
item.description: It's not exactly surprising that RAMaggeddon is making new tech hardware really expensive. But if you've been in the market for things like a new computer or tablet, this week has been filled with...

item.title: My trading bot said it was trading for four days... he was lying
item.description: Twenty-five days on Hyperliquid. Sixty-five closed trades. P&L: -$9.21. Turns out that was the smallest wrong thing about it. The landing page showed -$7.72 because it uses a different P&L formula...

item.title: Synthetic Data: The Hidden Ingredient That Made Modern LLMs Scale
item.description: Hello, I'm Shrijith Venkatramana. I'm building git-lrc, an AI code reviewer that runs on every commit. Star Us to help devs discover the project. Do give it a try and share your feedback for...

item.title: You won’t have long to get these iPad deals before Apple’s price hike
item.description: Apple just raised prices across its iPad and MacBook lineup. The good news is that many retailers are still selling their inventory at the old prices or far less, which means you can still score...

item.title: 🚀 Day 4 of Learning React: What Actually Makes React... React? (Hooks, useState, and State Explained)
item.description: 📌 Missed Day 3? I covered what actually happens after running npm run dev, how bundlers work, JSX, components, and props. You can read it here and then come back. I'll wait. ☕️ If someone asked me...

item.title: The CSV bug that compiles clean and breaks in production
item.description: Here is a bug I have shipped more than once. You have an export that turns records into CSV. The column list is just strings: import Papa from 'papaparse'; Papa.unparse(users, { columns: ['id',...

item.title: Beyond TinyPNG: Fast, Private, and Zero-Server Image Conversion
item.description: Have you ever found yourself hesitating before clicking "upload" on a third-party image compression site? You have a sensitive UI mockup, a proprietary dashboard screenshot, or internal company...

item.title: Your Vibe-Coded App Isn't Real Until It Has a URL
item.description: Here's a sentence that has quietly become a lie: "I built an app." You built something that runs on your screen. Maybe in a preview pane, maybe on localhost, maybe inside a chat window. It works....

item.title: Polestar has been muscled out of the US market
item.description: Polestar won't be allowed to sell its electric vehicles model year 2027 and beyond in the US after the federal government denied the company's request for authorization under a new rule banning...

item.title: Amazon S3, Object Stores, and the Directories That Don't Exist
item.description: S3 and Object Storage Most of us have used Amazon S3 at some point and may have wondered: If AWS provides S3, why are we using Linode Object Storage or MinIO instead? The reason is that Amazon S3...

item.title: Fugu hides the seams: multiple AIs, billed as a whole
item.description: Sakana AI's pitch with Fugu is unusual: stop chasing one bigger model, and instead train a model whose job is to direct other models. The seams between vendors are hidden, and you get back a...

item.title: I checked six LLM-as-judge tools against human labels. The scoreboard was the wrong thing to read.
item.description: Most LLM-as-judge comparisons rank tools by which one gives you a number fastest. That is the wrong axis. A judge you have not validated against human labels is not a measurement, it is a vibe...

item.title: Here’s a bunch of Prime Day deals on keyboards, mice, and other peripherals we like
item.description: RAMageddon has come for computers. The price of memory chips, hard drives, and solid state storage has skyrocketed. That’s led to price increases on desktop and laptop RAM, SSDs, spinning hard...

item.title: From Text to Morse Code - How Optical Signals Carry Information
item.description: Morse code is one of the oldest encoding systems still worth understanding today. It predates modern networking by over a century, yet it maps directly onto the same core ideas - binary states,...

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude is winning over paid consumers, a market owned by ChatGPT
item.description: Despite ChatGPT's commanding market lead, consumers who pay for AI have been increasingly choosing Anthropic's Claude, data shows.

item.title: This year’s Prime Day deals on Apple products are the best I’ve seen
item.description: Amazon’s Prime Day is now in its third day, and whether you’re looking for a new pair of wireless earbuds or a smartwatch, there’s a good chance you’ll find a discount. The Apple Watch Series 11...

item.title: 54 Prime Day steals under $25 you won’t want to miss
item.description: We at The Verge love a good gadget or tchotchke, especially when it’s not too expensive. But with the rising cost of just about everything these days, many once-cheap gadgets aren’t so affordable...

item.title: AI and Liability
item.description: Earlier this month, a German court ruled that Google is liable for its AI search summaries. Rejecting defenses like “users can check for themselves,” and that they generally know “that information...

item.title: Prime Day deals under $50 that are really worth it
item.description: If you’re looking for a good deal without spending hundreds, Prime Day discounts have pushed some of our favorite products under $50. You can pick up everything from Nintendo Switch games to...

item.title: Over 100 fresh tech deals from Prime Day day 3
item.description: It’s day three of Prime Day, folks. We’re nearly 75 percent of the way through Amazon’s four-day sales event, and unsurprisingly, many deals are still sticking around. There have been all kinds of...

item.title: General Intuition’s $2.3B bet that video games can train AI agents for the real world
item.description: General Intuition has raised $320 million to scale AI trained on millions of hours of gameplay, betting action data can help AI develop something closer to human intuition.

item.title: Xbox prices spike another $100 or more
item.description: Microsoft is increasing the prices of Xbox consoles again. Starting August 1st, 512GB models will be $100 more expensive, while 1TB models will be $150 more expensive. This means that the Xbox...

item.title: Databricks’ former AI chief thinks he can cut AI’s power bill by 1,000x
item.description: Un-0 is an image-generation system tool that shows for the first time how the company's technology can replicate conventional AI systems.

item.title: The Apple Watch Series 11 is $120 off for Prime Day, its all-time low price
item.description: We’ve seen a lot of great Prime Day deals on wearables this year, from the Fitbit Charge 6 to the latest Apple Watch SE. But if you’re looking for advanced health features, the Apple Watch Series...

item.title: Which tokens does a hybrid model predict better?
Our latest Google Finance upgrades, including a new app
item.description: The Google Finance logo, surrounded by elements of the user interface

item.title: Netris raises $15M Series A from a16z to help AI neoclouds go live faster
item.description: Netris provides software that runs on network switches, and offers a platform that helps neocloud operators reduce the time it takes to go live.

item.title: Repositioning retail for the AI era
item.description: Artificial intelligence is rapidly reshaping retail, but not in the ways consumers might immediately notice. The biggest transformation may not be flashy virtual try-ons or chatbot shopping...

item.title: Chrome Ad Blocker with 10M+ Installs Found with Dormant Script Injection Capability
item.description: An analysis of a popular Google Chrome ad block extension for YouTube has uncovered the ability to execute arbitrary JavaScript code. According to Island, the extension, named Adblock for YouTube...

item.title: 2 days left to save up to $190: Join 1,000+ founders and investors at TechCrunch Founder Summit
item.description: Two days left to lock in your spot at TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026 and save up to $190 before Early Bird rates expire on June 26 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register today.
